figures.caption: Figure 8: Standings and submission pages for GrandCode in Round 1089. The score corresponds to S(seperate), the score achieved by each solution as soon as it is ready. A – 498, B – 976, C1 – 1225, C2 – 944, D – 2088, E – 2519, F – 3346; S(seperate): 11596.

Figure 9: Problem G. Toothless (Round 1088, Div. 1+2): complete statement, sample I/O, and example illustrations. Each panel shows the grid before (left) and after (right) moves; dark cells are colored black.

Figure 5: An illustration of pipelined context parallelism for one block with 3 DeltaNet layers (L1, L2, L3) + 1 softmax attention layer with 4 CP ranks and 4 micro-batches for illustration purposes. In the DeltaNet phase, each GPU processes micro-batches (MB) in a staggered pipeline, passing the recurrent state forward; startup and drain bubbles (gray) are confined to the triangular corners. The softmax attention layer is executed with synchronized all-to-all communication at full utilization.
Figure 6: Standings and submission pages for GrandCode in the three live Codeforces contests. The score corresponds to S(joint), which is based on the full set of submissions in a single account.

Figure 3: Hypothesis generation and small-scale verification. The agent first proposes a compact characterization (kmax = maxv(last(v)− first(v))), then generates small random instances, computes the exact answer via a brute-force solver that enumerates all tuples (i, j, k) ∈ S(b), and compares against the hypothesized value. A mismatch triggers hypothesis revision and only validated hypotheses are promoted to solution synthesis.
Figure 4: Examples of contest figures whose visual structure is difficult to capture with text-only descriptions.

Figure 7: Standings and submission pages for GrandCode. The score corresponds to S(seperate), the score achieved by each solution as soon as it is ready. (a) Round 1087. A–492, B–685, C–1476, D–1603, E–2169, F–2844; S(seperate): 9269. (b) Round 1088. A–486, B–1234, C1–1230, C2–959, D–1968, E–2054, F–2895, G–2888, H–2797; S(seperate): 16511.

Figure 2: Overview of the full pipeline. In post-training, we continue training on noisy competitiveprogramming data, perform supervised fine-tuning on reference solutions, train auxiliary hypothesis generation policy πhypothesis and summarization policy πsummary and jointly optimize the system with multi-component RL. At test/online-contest time, the model uses direct generation for easy cases, and an online test-time RL loop for harder cases.
